_schema: Locked-in animal routes per location. Generated by multi_template.run_search; preserved here so future sessions can reproduce a chosen result without re-running Optuna. Values are full-precision floats from the v2 summary JSONs — do not round them, the route is sensitive to lat/lon snap-to-nearest-node.

router._notes: revisit_penalty_m=0 lets each leg/trunk splice loop trace as a clean out-and-back. The appendage out-and-back structure is encoded by templates_loader._silhouette_outline (RETR_TREE splice of inner contours) — do NOT try to re-derive anatomy from scratch in a follow-up session, that work was abandoned on this branch because the outline already contains the right structure. beta=6 tightens the per-leg corridor around the template line.

search._notes: Length penalty off; rotation clipped near canonical orientation so the elephant doesn't end up on its side. Optuna picks scale/placement; we pin template by visual cherry-pick. Iconic QD templates only: ELE-Q01, ELE-Q07, ELE-Q08, ELE-Q15, ELE-Q18.

elephant_st_albans._chosen_over_top1: Top-1 was ELE-Q07 with a 66km route at scale=7.4km (messy interior, too long). Rank-4 trades some Optuna fidelity score for a much cleaner ~37km elephant at canonical orientation with visible leg/trunk spikes — overrides Optuna's objective which doesn't capture 'looks like an elephant'.
